column.name: Actual_Address
column.description: Raw geocoded or user-entered location detail for the client, often a full street address or latitude/longitude string. Use as the most granular address text but expect inconsistent formatting and occasional coordinates.

column.name: AddCity
column.description: Client’s city/town captured for address reporting and territory mapping (e.g., Mohniya, Pune, Gurugram). Typically used for city-level aggregations and may contain spelling variants.

column.name: AddState
column.description: Client’s state/region as captured in the address (e.g., Bihar, Odisha, Mah-Pune), sometimes using internal state codes or region labels. Interpret as a geographic attribute but standardize before statewide analysis.

column.name: AssignEmp
column.description: Employee code/name intended to represent the currently assigned field employee for the client; often stored as a string and frequently appears as 'null'. Treat as optional/dirty assignment metadata and prefer Emp_Id/BeatOwnerEmp_Id when analyzing ownership.

column.name: BeatDate
column.description: Datetime (stored as text) when the client was mapped/updated to a beat or when the beat assignment was recorded. Parse to a timestamp for recency analyses and be cautious of inconsistent text formats.

column.name: BeatDistributorId
column.description: Identifier (as text) for the distributor linked to the beat/client mapping, often aligning with the distributor in ClientSourceId/ClientSourceCode. Use to join to distributor masters but normalize datatype and handle blanks.

column.name: BeatId
column.description: Unique identifier (stored as text) for the beat/route to which the client belongs. Use for beat-level coverage and routing analysis; may need casting to numeric.

column.name: BeatOwnerEmp_Id
column.description: Employee identifier (stored as text) for the beat owner responsible for the route/clients. Use as the primary beat ownership key when measuring beat responsibility, but cast and validate against Emp_Id domains.

column.name: BeatOwnerName
column.description: Name of the employee who owns the beat (e.g., Vishal Kumar). Use for display/reporting; do not use as a join key due to spelling/case variations.

column.name: BeatType
column.description: Cadence/frequency of beat visits such as Weekly, Fortnight, or Monthly. Useful for expected coverage planning and compliance comparisons versus actual visits.

column.name: Beats
column.description: Human-readable beat/route name or description (e.g., 'Kalamboli', 'Digapahandi + Dengaosta'). Use for reporting labels; not guaranteed unique and may include multiple localities.

column.name: ClientDivision
column.description: Client’s division/segment within the business if applicable; commonly blank in samples. Treat as an optional segmentation field and validate population before using in filters.

column.name: ClientSSId
column.description: Identifier for the client’s source system or selling-stockist/distributor system entity (bigint). Use to link to upstream source masters (e.g., stockist/distributor systems) and to deduplicate cross-system entities.

column.name: ClientSSName
column.description: Name corresponding to ClientSSId, typically the selling stockist/agency entity (e.g., 'Friends Traders'). Use for display and grouping at the stockist level, not as a stable key.

column.name: ClientSource
column.description: Text name of the immediate source/partner associated with the client (often a distributor or outlet source label). Use as descriptive metadata; prefer ClientSourceId/ClientSourceType for structured analysis.

column.name: ClientSourceCode
column.description: Source code for the client’s source entity, often matching distributor codes but sometimes blank. Use as an alternate identifier for source mapping and handle missing/empty values.

column.name: ClientSourceId
column.description: Numeric identifier (bigint) of the client source entity, commonly a distributor ID. Use as the primary structured key for tying clients to their source/distributor hierarchy.

column.name: ClientSourceType
column.description: Type/category of the client source such as 'Distributor'. Use to distinguish upstream entity types when multiple source kinds exist.

column.name: Client_Id
column.description: Primary unique identifier (bigint) for the retail client/outlet record and the main join key to transactions/visits; indexed for faster lookups. Analysts should treat this as the authoritative client key for deduplication and entity linking.

column.name: Client_Name
column.description: Display name of the retailer/client outlet (e.g., 'Mahaveer Beauty'). Use for reporting/search; not unique and subject to casing/spelling inconsistencies.

column.name: Client_Type
column.description: Business classification of the client such as 'Retailer'. Use for segment filters and to separate outlets from other client categories if present.

column.name: Created_By
column.description: Identifier (stored as text) of the user/employee who created the client master record. Useful for audit and adoption tracking; may require casting to match employee IDs.

column.name: Creation_Date
column.description: Datetime (stored as text) when the client record was created in the system. Parse to a timestamp for cohorting and data freshness; format varies by row.

column.name: Designation
column.description: Sales role/designation of the associated employee (e.g., TSI, TSO, Sr.TSI). Use for role-based performance analysis and hierarchy reporting.

column.name: Division
column.description: Business division under which the client is managed (e.g., 'Retail'). Use as a high-level organizational segment for reporting and access scoping.

column.name: DstrCity
column.description: Distributor city or the servicing distribution city tied to the client/beat (e.g., Brahmapur, Jabalpur). Use for distribution footprint analysis; may differ from AddCity (client location).

column.name: ECO
column.description: Text flag indicating ECO status (e.g., Yes/No). Treat as a descriptive duplicate of IsECO and prefer IsECO for boolean logic.

column.name: EmpState
column.description: State/region of the employee responsible for the client/beat, often aligned with operational territory. Use for territory reporting but standardize codes similarly to AddState.

column.name: EmpZone
column.description: Higher-level geographic zone for the employee/territory (e.g., West, South, Upper North). Use for zonal rollups and management reporting.

column.name: Emp_Id
column.description: Employee identifier (bigint) associated with the client record (often the field owner/primary responsible); indexed for faster filtering. Use as the main key to join employee master data and attribute outlet ownership.

column.name: Emp_Name
column.description: Name of the employee corresponding to Emp_Id. Use for display; avoid using as a join key due to variability.

column.name: FirstCheckInDate
column.description: Date when the first check-in/visit was recorded for the client. Use to understand onboarding/activation timing and to distinguish newly created vs first-visited outlets.

column.name: Folder_Name
column.description: Optional folder/grouping label for organizing clients, often 'null' or empty. Treat as non-critical metadata unless your process uses folder-based segmentation.

column.name: FullAddress
column.description: Concatenated full address string assembled from components (city/state/country/zip), used for display and geocoding. Expect trailing commas and inconsistent component order; prefer structured fields for analytics.

column.name: Function
column.description: Optional functional tag for the employee/client context, frequently null/blank. Use only if populated consistently for role/function segmentation.

column.name: HQState
column.description: Headquarter state/territory for the employee/client mapping, often mirroring EmpState/AddState. Use as a territory assignment attribute and validate against official HQ mappings.

column.name: IsECO
column.description: Boolean indicator of ECO status for the client/route (True/False). Use for filtering ECO-enabled outlets and prefer over the text ECO field.

column.name: IsREC
column.description: Boolean indicator of REC status (True/False), likely denoting recommended/recognized/enrolled category for the outlet. Use as a program eligibility/participation flag and confirm business definition before KPI use.

column.name: Label
column.description: Client label/code used operationally (e.g., '500040', 'GS10504115', 'CAN595753'). Use as an external-facing or legacy identifier; may not be unique across all divisions without context.

column.name: LastOrderDate
column.description: Date of the most recent order placed by the client. Use for recency/active-inactive classification and churn/risk metrics.

column.name: LastVisitBy
column.description: Identifier of the employee who last visited the client (stored as text). Use to attribute the most recent touchpoint, but cast/validate against employee IDs.

column.name: LastVisitDate
column.description: Date of the last recorded visit/check-in stored as text (often dd/mm/yyyy). Parse carefully with locale awareness and treat blanks/nulls as no-visit recorded.

column.name: LastVisitEmp_Id
column.description: Employee identifier (stored as text) representing the employee tied to the last visit event. Use for visit attribution and reconcile with LastVisitBy when inconsistencies occur.

column.name: OTPVerifyDate
column.description: Datetime (stored as text) when OTP verification occurred for the client, often null. Use for compliance/audit of OTP-based verification steps and treat null as not verified via OTP.

column.name: PositionCode
column.description: Organizational position/territory code for the responsible employee (e.g., 'BH_HR_FF1'). Use to map to hierarchy/territory structures and for position-based reporting.

column.name: Reference
column.description: Free-text reference or remark field for client creation/mapping, commonly blank. Use as qualitative metadata only and avoid using for structured analysis.

column.name: Speciality
column.description: Optional speciality/category tag for the client, often null/blank in samples. Use for niche segmentation if populated (e.g., outlet type specialization).

column.name: TLName
column.description: Name of the team leader/manager for the associated employee. Use for managerial rollups and display; not a stable key without an accompanying TL ID.

column.name: Varified
column.description: Verification/approval status text (note spelling), such as 'Waiting for approval..' or detailed approval remarks. Use to determine master-data approval state, but standardize statuses (approved/pending/rejected) from the free text.

column.name: pId
column.description: Internal surrogate/processing ID (bigint) for the record, likely used for ETL/load tracking rather than business identity. Use for technical deduplication or incremental load auditing, not as the client key.
